name: Iteration from bad output
prompt: Improve the existing csv-cleaner skill in this repository. It currently over-triggers, has a bloated SKILL.md, and creates references for tiny rules. Make it precise and compact while preserving the useful behavior: clean CSV rows by trimming whitespace, normalizing empty cells, and preserving column order. Run structural validation before finishing.

judge.check: The agent updated skills/csv-cleaner rather than creating an unrelated new skill.
The description becomes narrower and no longer claims broad analytics, spreadsheet, or generic table-editing behavior.
The runtime guidance is materially shorter and more actionable, with unnecessary references removed unless clearly justified.
The improvement generalizes from the negative evidence instead of merely patching words from this prompt.
The transcript or final response shows structural validation was run.

setup.command:
printf '%s
' '---' 'name: csv-cleaner' 'description: Clean CSV files, spreadsheets, analytics exports, tables, tabular data, reports, dashboards, and anything involving rows or columns.' '---' '' '# CSV Cleaner' '' 'Use this skill for any tabular data task.' '' 'Read references/rules.md for basic trimming rules.' '' 'Always explain every transformation in detail.' > skills/csv-cleaner/SKILL.md

printf '%s
' '# CSV Cleaner Specification' '' '## Intent' '' 'Clean CSV row data while preserving column order.' > skills/csv-cleaner/SPEC.md

printf '%s
' '# Rules' '' '- Trim whitespace.' '' '- Normalize empty cells.' > skills/csv-cleaner/references/rules.md
